hard_prerequisites.note: Re-derived correct-position sequence from quiz.gift matches sidecar exactly across all 10 MCQs.

length_bias.note: 2 of 10 MCQs have a correct answer >20% longer than the distractor mean (char length, feedback stripped). Rate (0.20) is below the 0.40 fail threshold and close to the 0.25 four-option baseline; no action required.

distractor_plausibility.note: All distractors spot-checked encode genuine beginner misconceptions traceable to corpus content: type-conversion/ADT conflation (q1), identity vs class/instance confusion (q2), constructor-timing and shared-vs-instance-attribute errors (q3, q5, q7, q8), self-binding/self-as-class confusion (q4, q6, q10), and naming-convention mix-ups (q9). None are absurd/free-point options.

learning_objectives.lo: LO1: ADT is state+behaviour bundled together, why that's useful
LO2: Distinguish class (blueprint) from object/instance; create instances
LO3: Constructor (__init__) initializing instance attributes; instance vs class attribute distinction
LO4: Instance methods, correct use of self, calling methods on an object

objective_coverage.note: q8/q9/q10 extend into corpus Section 6 (Implementation) and Section 8 (Best Practices) material (naming conventions, missing-self TypeError, uninitialized-attribute AttributeError) rather than Section 3's LO wording verbatim, but each is explicitly grounded in a cited corpus section and fuzzy-maps (>=0.7) to LO2/LO3/LO4 respectively — not treated as orphans.

verdict: Pass — positional bias max_share 0.30 (<=0.40), length bias longest_correct_rate 0.20 (<=0.40), distractor absurd_rate 0.00 (<=0.25), all 4 Learning Objectives covered with no orphan questions and no meta/gift desync.
